name: CONGENITAL PULMONARY AIRWAY MALFORMATION (CPAM
definition: CONGENITAL CYSTIC ADENOMATOID MALFORMATION (CCAM) congenital abnormality of the lung characterized by an intralobar mass of disorganized ACINAR DYSPLASIA OF THE LUNG (previous designation) severe global arrest of lung development

demographics.Prevalence: 1÷25,000-35,000 pregnancies; 25% of congenital lung disorders; 30-40% of all
demographics.Path: proliferation of bronchial structures at the expense of alveolar saccular development;
demographics.Histo: acinar dysgenesis / dysplasia = no cysts / very small ones (<0.5 cm)

hyperintense uni- / multilocular lesion with discrete walls on T2WI
mass of variable density ± mediastinal shift on postnatal radiograph
round soft-tissue mass gradually filling with air on neonatal radiographs ← delayed
